text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-hook-form repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: for a field array whose resolver can report both a root-level error on the array itself (e.g. "needs at least N items") and nested per-index errors on individual items in the same validation pass, calling trigger() alongside a field-array action such as remove() can wipe out the nested per-index errors, leaving only (or none of) the root error behind.

Concretely: set up a form with useFieldArray where the resolver returns an errors object shaped like { test: { type: 'min', message: '...', 0: {...}, 2: {...} } } — a plain object that carries the root error's type/message directly on itself while also carrying nested errors keyed by numeric index (this is the shape useFieldArray's own internal effect leaves errors in after an array mutation). Trigger validation once — both the root error and the nested per-index errors render correctly. Now call a field-array mutator like remove(i) followed immediately by trigger('test') again. After this second validation pass, the previously-visible nested per-index error(s) disappear from formState.errors, even though the resolver is still reporting them and the indices have simply shifted after the removal.

Fix the library source so that when an existing field-array error is already a plain object carrying nested numeric-indexed errors (not a bare root-only error, and not already an array), it is not coerced/wrapped in a way that hides those nested indices — the root error info and the nested per-index errors must both remain independently readable from formState.errors after subsequent trigger() calls. Do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: dependencies are already installed (via `npm install -g pnpm@10.34.5 && CYPRESS_INSTALL_BINARY=0 pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts`). Run the relevant tests with `node_modules/.bin/jest --config ./scripts/jest/jest.config.js src/__tests__/useFieldArray.test.tsx`. Fix the library source; do not just edit tests.

message: `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.
`--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.

text: I’ll trace the field-array error normalization and the `trigger`/mutation interaction, then patch the library code and run the focused `useFieldArray` Jest suite. I’ll preserve any unrelated worktree changes.

The relevant path is the shared `updateFieldArrayRootError` helper, used both by resolver-driven validation and by the field-array action effect. I’m reading its callers and existing tests now to verify the exact data-shape invariant before editing.

15d17628 🐞 fix(setValues): emit whole-form change without stale name/type (#13450)
989cbff4 🚗 perf(setValues): thread skipClone through setFieldValue (#13448)
8f14b211 🚗 perf(setValues): skip redundant per-field deep clones (#13445)

75fc3a52 🐞 fix native validation tooltip suppression caused by duplicate submit-error focus (#13432)

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "git blame -L 1,80 src/logic/updateFieldArrayRootError.ts && git log -S'convertToArrayPayload(get(errors, name))' --all --oneline -- src/logic/updateFieldArrayRootError.ts"

/bin/bash -lc "cat src/utils/convertToArrayPayload.ts && sed -n '1120,1170p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts && sed -n '1380,1530p' src/__tests__/useFieldArray.test.tsx && sed -n '3860,4010p' src/__tests__/useFieldArray.test.tsx"

text: The failure mechanism is confirmed: `updateFieldArrayRootError` always converts a non-array existing value into `[value]`. After the field-array effect writes the resolver’s plain `{ type, message, 0, 2 }` object back at `test`, the next `trigger('test')` wraps that object and moves the numeric entries under index `0`. I’m checking local utility conventions and type expectations, then I’ll add a narrowly-scoped preservation branch.

  BLUR: 'blur',

  CHANGE: 'change',

  TRIGGER: 'trigger',
  VALID: 'valid',
} as const;

export const VALIDATION_MODE = {
  onBlur: 'onBlur',
  onChange: 'onChange',

  onTouched: 'onTouched',

} as const;

/bin/bash -lc "rg -n \"isObject|isPlainObject|isNumeric|numeric\" src/utils src/logic | head -100 && ls src/utils | head -80 && sed -n '1,100p' src/utils/isObject.ts 2>/dev/null || true"

text: I’ll update only the shared normalization helper. The preservation condition will require a plain object with at least one integer-like key, so root-only resolver errors retain their existing behavior while array-shaped errors and mixed root-plus-index objects remain readable at both levels. I’ll also add a regression test covering `remove()` followed immediately by `trigger('test')`.

The source fix and regression case are in place. The test intentionally starts with whole-form `trigger()` (which preserves the resolver object), then performs `remove(1)` and `trigger('test')` in the same handler so it exercises the reported transition and verifies root plus shifted nested errors.

text: The focused regression exposed one additional edge in the test setup: the resolver’s second response has the shifted numeric key, so the helper must preserve the plain-object representation while using the current resolver payload for its numeric entries. I’m tightening that merge behavior and guarding the plain-object check for the normal `undefined` initial state; the first version also showed that the existing `isPlainObject` utility assumes a non-null object.

text:
Fixed the field-array error normalization bug in [updateFieldArrayRootError.ts](/home/user/workspace/src/logic/updateFieldArrayRootError.ts:17). Mixed plain-object errors now preserve numeric nested errors and root metadata across subsequent triggers.

Added regression coverage in [useFieldArray.test.tsx](/home/user/workspace/src/__tests__/useFieldArray.test.tsx:461).
